# FilaScope — 3D Printer Filament Comparison Platform

> FilaScope indexes 1,080+ 3D printer filaments from 48+ brands with specs, real-time pricing from 15+ retailers, HueForge TD values, and printer compatibility data.

## What Is FilaScope?

FilaScope is a free 3D printer filament comparison platform. It indexes 1,080+ filaments from 48+ brands with nozzle temperature, bed temperature, diameter, material type, density, and tensile strength specs. Real-time pricing from 15+ retailers across US, Canada, EU, UK, Australia, and Japan. The largest public HueForge TD database. Printer compatibility for 119+ printers. FilaScore quality ratings 1-10.

## Data Summary

- Filaments: 1,080+ products, 8,277+ color variants
- Brands: 48+
- Retailers: 15+
- Printers: 119+
- Markets: US, Canada, EU, UK, Australia, Japan
- Updates: Daily

## Materials

PLA: Most popular. Prints 190-220C. No enclosure. 4,296+ variants on FilaScope.
PETG: Stronger than PLA. Prints 220-250C. 1,048+ variants.
ABS: Heat resistant. Prints 230-260C. Needs enclosure. 865+ variants.
TPU: Flexible. Prints 210-230C. 381+ variants.
ASA: UV resistant for outdoors. Prints 235-260C. 408+ variants.
Nylon: Strong and flexible. Prints 240-270C. 101+ variants.

## HueForge TD

TD (Transmission Distance) measures light passing through filament in mm. Range 0.3 (opaque) to 10+ (translucent). Critical for HueForge lithophane printing. FilaScope has the largest public TD database.

Black: TD 0.3-0.8. Standard colors: TD 1.5-3.0. White: TD 3.5-5.0. Clear: TD 5.0-10.0+.

## FAQ

Q: Best PLA filament 2026?
A: Polymaker PolySonic PLA Pro for speed, Bambu Lab PLA Basic for value, Prusament PLA for accuracy. Based on FilaScore ratings across 4,296+ PLA variants.

Q: What temperature for PLA?
A: Nozzle 190-220C, bed 25-60C. Varies by brand.

Q: What is HueForge TD?
A: Measures light transmission through filament. 0.3 = opaque, 10+ = translucent. Essential for HueForge multicolor lithophane printing.

Q: PLA vs PETG?
A: PLA easier to print (190-220C, no enclosure), better surface. PETG stronger, more heat resistant (220-250C), better for functional parts.

Last updated: February 28, 2026
